name: Nozick, Tabarrok, and Dominant Assurance Contracts
abstract: This article explores Robert Nozick's complex arguments regarding wealth redistribution, initially presenting a case for compulsory giving based on the free rider problem and fear of exploitation. However, Nozick ultimately rejects compulsory schemes due to their violation of individual rights. The article highlights Nozick's innovative solution: incentivizing voluntary charitable contributions through contingent contracts, an idea formalized by Alex Tabarrok as "dominant assurance contracts." These contracts, akin to modern crowdfunding, enable the private provision of public goods by ensuring funds are utilized only if a set threshold is met, thus avoiding exploitation and respecting individual autonomy.
